en.comprehension: An attempt to determine the number of languages in the world is affected by other factors. A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days, as unexplored regions of the world begin to be opened up. The discovery does not usually take place straight away. Often there are similarities with an already known language which make the investigators assume that what they have found is just a dialect of that language. Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language. It takes a language survey to establish the facts, and there are still many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun. The people may be known, but the identity of their language may not be. Because many such peoples are bilingual or multilingual, and converse with outsiders in lingua franca, it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all.
en.content: Which one of the following is NOT the reason for the difficulty in estimating the number of languages of the world ?

options.content: New languages continue to be discovered
New languages are considered a dialect of a known language
People who speak the language do not claim their language

en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option 4.

Key Points

The question asks which option is NOT a reason for the difficulty in estimating the number of languages according to the passage.

Let's examine each option against the text:

New languages continue to be discovered: The passage states, "A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days…" This is a reason for the difficulty in estimation.

There are still unexplored regions: The passage mentions, "…as unexplored regions of the world begin to be opened up." This contributes directly to the discovery of new languages, making estimation challenging. This is a reason.

New languages are considered a dialect of a known language: The passage explicitly states, "Often there are similarities with an already known language which make the investigators assume that what they have found is just a dialect of that language. Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language." This is a reason for difficulty in initial identification.

People who speak the language do not claim their language: The passage indicates, "Because many such peoples are bilingual or multilingual, and converse with outsiders in lingua franca, it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all." This implies that linguists may not realize a language exists due to multilingualism and the use of a lingua franca, but it does not suggest that speakers do not claim their language. The passage does not mention speakers actively refusing to claim their language, hence this is NOT explicitly stated as a reason for difficulty.

Thus, the correct answer is Option 4.

en.content: Which one of the following is a way to establish the discovery of a new language ?

options.content: By establishing contact over a period of time with the speakers of the language
By finding the similarities with the language with other languages
By comparing with the lingua franca of the region
By conducting a survey of all languages of the region

en.explanation:
The passage explains: "Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language." This means that it takes a while, after interacting with the people, for the significant differences to become clear enough to classify it as a distinct language.

So, looking at the options, Option 1 aligns perfectly with this description.

Let's briefly consider the others:

Option 2 is actually what might lead investigators to think it's not a new language initially (because of similarities).

Option 3 isn't mentioned as the way to establish a new language, but rather the use of lingua franca can sometimes hide the existence of an ethnic language.

Option 4, conducting a survey, is mentioned as a way "to establish the facts" which would certainly be part of the formal process, but the text specifically points to the "period of contact" as the point where the distinction becomes clear enough to consider it a different language.

Therefore, the text highlights Option 1: By establishing contact over a period of time with the speakers of the language as a key way to establish the discovery of a new language by realizing its significant difference.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Reading Comprehension • English
en.excerpt: Which one of the following is a way to establish the discovery of a new language ?

en.comprehension: An attempt to determine the number of languages in the world is affected by other factors. A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days, as unexplored regions of the world begin to be opened up. The discovery does not usually take place straight away. Often there are similarities with an already known language which make the investigators assume that what they have found is just a dialect of that language. Only after a considerable period of contact does it transpire that the speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language. It takes a language survey to establish the facts, and there are still many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun. The people may be known, but the identity of their language may not be. Because many such peoples are bilingual or multilingual, and converse with outsiders in lingua franca, it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all.
en.content: Which one of the following statements is correct ?

options.content: Language surveys have been conducted by all nations
There is hardly any language to be discovered in the world
All the languages are either complete languages or dialects of other major languages
No new language needs to be discovered since all languages are known to the world

en.explanation:
The text says:

New languages are still being discovered ("A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days").

It's hard to distinguish new languages from dialects initially ("Often there are similarities… assume that what they have found is just a dialect… speech is so different that it has to be considered a different language").

Language surveys are needed, but many countries have incomplete or no surveys ("there are still many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun").

Languages can be hidden by people being bilingual and using lingua francas ("it may take a while before linguists come to realize that there is an ethnic language there at all").

Now let's check the options against these points:

Option 1: Language surveys have been conducted by all nations.

The text says "many countries where such surveys are incomplete or have not even begun." This directly contradicts Option 1.

Option 2: There is hardly any language to be discovered in the world.

The text says "A few new languages do continue to be discovered, even these days". "A few" continuing to be discovered suggests there are still some, perhaps not a massive number, but definitely more than "hardly any," especially considering the incomplete surveys mentioned. This statement seems to contradict the text.

Option 3: All the languages are either complete languages or dialects of other major languages.

The text describes the process of trying to figure out if newly encountered speech is a "dialect of that language" or "so different that it has to be considered a different language." This implies that the distinction investigators are grappling with is between something being a separate "language" and being a "dialect" of another. While the text doesn't explicitly state this is a universal truth about all linguistic varieties, it frames the challenge of discovery and identification using this exact dichotomy. It reflects the operational categories being used in the scenario described.

Option 4: No new language needs to be discovered since all languages are known to the world.

The text explicitly says "A few new languages do continue to be discovered" and mentions incomplete surveys and hidden languages. This statement is clearly contradicted by the text.

Based on the text provided, Options 1 and 4 are definitely incorrect. Option 2 is also unlikely to be correct given that new languages are still being discovered. Option 3 best reflects the core distinction that the text describes investigators struggling with when trying to identify and count languages: determining if something is a distinct language or a dialect.

So, the statement that is correct based on the text is Option 3.

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech with four options. One of the options converts the direct or indirect speech into indirect or direct correctly. Select the correct option and mark your response on the Answer Sheet.
en.content:
Convert from direct speech to indirect speech :
Charu said to her friend, “I want you to be here at 6.00 p.m. tomorrow for the meeting.”

options.content: Charu told her friend that she wanted her to be there at 6.00 p.m. the next day for the meeting
Charu told her friend that she wanted her to be there at 6.00 p.m. tomorrow for the meeting
Charu requested her friend that she wanted her to be there at 6.00 p.m. tomorrow for the meeting
Charu told her friend that she will want her to be here at 6.00 p.m. the next day for the meeting

en.explanation:
The correct choice is Option 1.

Reasoning:

“said to” becomes “told”

“want” shifts to past tense “wanted” after “told”

“tomorrow” changes to “the next day”

So we get:

Charu told her friend that she wanted her to be there at 6.00 p.m. the next day for the meeting.

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech with four options. One of the options converts the direct or indirect speech into indirect or direct correctly. Select the correct option and mark your response on the Answer Sheet.
en.content:
Convert from direct speech to indirect speech :
Nitin said to his brother, “What a beautiful painting it is ?”

options.content: Nitin wondered to his brother that what a beautiful painting it was
Nitin wondered before his brother that it was a beautiful painting
Nitin exclaimed that it is a beautiful painting
Nitin asked his brother whether it was beautiful painting

en.explanation:
The original sentence is an exclamatory statement that expresses admiration for the painting, despite the unconventional use of a question mark. The key to converting exclamatory sentences to indirect speech involves several steps:

Change the Reporting Verb: The verb 'said to' should be replaced with verbs like 'exclaimed', 'remarked', or 'wondered', which reflect the emotion expressed.

Convert to Declarative Form: The exclamatory sentence is transformed into a declarative statement. For expressions like "What a + adjective + noun + it is/was", it converts to "it was a very + adjective + noun" or "it was a + adjective + noun".

Backshift in Tense: The present tense 'is' changes to the past tense 'was'.

Use of 'that': The conjunction 'that' is typically used to introduce the reported speech.

Analysis of Options:

Option 1: Nitin wondered to his brother that what a beautiful painting it was.

This has redundant phrasing with "that what a" and "wondered to his brother" is awkward.

Option 2: Nitin wondered before his brother that it was a beautiful painting.

Although "wondered before his brother" sounds slightly awkward, it correctly translates "What a beautiful painting it is" to "it was a beautiful painting", and backshifts the tense from 'is' to 'was'.

Option 3: Nitin exclaimed that it is a beautiful painting.

Incorrect because the tense 'is' should be backshifted to 'was'.

Option 4: Nitin asked his brother whether it was beautiful painting.

Incorrect because the original sentence is not a question, making 'asked' and 'whether' inappropriate.

Correct Conversion:

The sentence correctly converted to indirect speech is: Nitin wondered before his brother that it was a beautiful painting.

en.subTitle: Improvement of Sentence • English

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a sentence in direct or indirect speech with four options. One of the options converts the direct or indirect speech into indirect or direct correctly. Select the correct option and mark your response on the Answer Sheet.
en.content:
Convert from indirect speech to direct speech :
The teacher asked her students why they had been quiet in the previous class.

options.content: The teacher asked her students, “Why were they keeping quiet in the previous class ?”
The teacher said to her students, “Why were you quiet in the previous class ?”
The teacher said to her students, “Why had you been quiet in the previous class ?”
The teacher said to her students, “Why were you quiet in previous class ?”

en.explanation:
Key Points

When converting indirect speech to direct speech, we need to reverse the changes that were made when converting from direct to indirect speech.

Given the original sentence in indirect speech: "The teacher asked her students why they had been quiet in the previous class," here’s how we convert it:

Reporting Verb: "Asked" in indirect speech indicates a question in direct speech. It can be reverted to "said to" or "asked."

Pronoun Change: "They" (referring to the students) changes back to "you."

Tense Change: "Had been quiet" (Past Perfect) usually reverts to "were quiet" (Simple Past) in direct speech, as it is the most natural form for asking about a past state.

Time/Place Adverb Change: "The previous class" remains "the previous class" or could change to "the last class."

Question Structure: Retain the 'why' question format.

Evaluating the Options:

"The teacher asked her students, “Why were they keeping quiet in the previous class?”

Incorrect pronoun ("they" instead of "you") and verb tense ("keeping quiet" instead of "were quiet").

"The teacher said to her students, “Why were you quiet in the previous class?”

Correct structure: uses "you," the tense "were quiet" (Simple Past) corresponds to "had been quiet" (Past Perfect) in indirect speech, and "the previous class" is retained. This option is grammatically correct and natural.

"The teacher said to her students, “Why had you been quiet in the previous class?”

While "had been quiet" reflects the Past Perfect, the Simple Past "were quiet" is usually the original form that converts to Past Perfect in indirect speech unless the sequence of events necessitates it. Less natural for a direct question.

"The teacher said to her students, “Why were you quiet in previous class?”

Incorrect due to missing "the" before "previous class."

Correct Sentence:

The teacher said to her students, “Why were you quiet in the previous class?”

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Improvement of Sentence • English
en.excerpt:
Convert from indirect speech to direct speech :
The teacher asked her students why they had been quiet in the previous class.

en.comprehension: Directions : Following items have four sentences, one of which is correct. Find the correct option and mark your response in the Answer Sheet.
en.content: Which one of the following sentences is correct ?

options.content: He is considered as the brightest intellectual in the country
He is considered as one among the brightest intellectual in the country
He is considered as one among the brighter intellectual in the country
He is considered as one of the intellectual in the country

en.explanation:
Key Points

Let's analyze each sentence to determine its correctness:

He is considered as the brightest intellectual in the country.

The use of "considered as" can sometimes be debated due to redundancy—often "considered" is preferred without "as" when assigning a role or quality (e.g., "He is considered the leader"). However, "considered as" is not strictly incorrect in all contexts.

"The brightest intellectual" is grammatically correct, as "intellectual" is a singular noun referring to one person.

This sentence is the most grammatically sound among the given options.

He is considered as one among the brightest intellectual in the country.

Incorrect: The phrase "one among the brightest" must be followed by a plural noun. It should be "intellectuals" (plural) because he is one person selected from a group of the brightest individuals.

He is considered as one among the brighter intellectual in the country.

Incorrect: "Brighter" is a comparative adjective, typically used for comparing two things. For comparisons within a group within the entire country, the superlative "brightest" is needed.

Incorrect: Similar to option 2, "intellectual" should be "intellectuals" (plural).

He is considered as one of the intellectual in the country.

Incorrect: The phrase "one of the" must always be followed by a plural noun. It should be "intellectuals" (plural).

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

en.comprehension: Directions : Following items have four sentences, one of which is correct. Find the correct option and mark your response in the Answer Sheet.
en.content: Which one of the following sentences is correct ?

options.content: No other mountain is taller than the Himalayas
No other mountain is tall than the Himalayas
No other mountains taller than the Himalayas
No other mountain is taller then the Himalayas

en.explanation:
Key Points

Let's analyze each sentence to determine its correctness:

1) No other mountain is taller than the Himalayas

"No other mountain is" correctly uses singular subject-verb agreement.

"Taller than" is the correct comparative form used for comparison.

This sentence is grammatically correct.

2) No other mountain is tall than the Himalayas

Incorrect: "Tall than" is wrong. When making a comparison with "than," the comparative adjective "taller" must be used.

3) No other mountains taller than the Himalayas

Incorrect: This is a sentence fragment because it lacks a main verb (e.g., "are").

4) No other mountain is taller then the Himalayas

Incorrect: "Then" is an adverb of time or sequence. The correct word for comparison is "than."

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items, a pair of words is provided. You are required to select the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both the words and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: Confident and Confidant

options.content: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend
Confidant means certain and confident means close friend
Confident means belief and confidant means an emissary
Confidant means assurance and confidant means intimate person

en.explanation:
Confident (with a 't' at the end) is usually an adjective. It describes a feeling of certainty about something or about yourself. Like, you might feel confident about passing an exam, or you might be a confident public speaker.

Confidant (with a 't' at the end for a male, or sometimes 'confidante' with 'te' for a female, but 'confidant' is often used for both) is a noun. It refers to a person that you trust deeply, often someone you share secrets or private matters with. They are your trusted companion or close friend.

Now let's look at the options:

Option 1: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend

Yes, "Confident" means certain or sure of yourself.

Yes, a "Confidant" is someone you're close to and trust with secrets, like a close friend. This option seems spot on.

Option 2: Confidant means certain and confident means close friend

No, "Confidant" is a person, not the feeling of certainty.

No, "Confident" describes a state of mind or attitude, not a person.

Option 3: Confident means belief and confidant means an emissary

"Confident" does involve belief (in oneself or something), but "certain" is a more direct description.

"Confidant" is definitely not an emissary (a messenger or representative).

Option 4: Confidant means assurance and confidant means intimate person

This option has a typo, using "confidant" twice. Even if we interpret the first part as "Confident means assurance", while related, "certain" is better. The second part, "confidant means intimate person", is a good description, but the option structure is flawed, and the first part isn't the best fit anyway.

Based on the standard meanings, Option 1 captures the distinction perfectly.

So, the most appropriate description is:

Option 1: Confident means certain and confidant means close friend

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items, a pair of words is provided. You are required to select the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both the words and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: Accept and Except

options.content: Accept means apart from and except means recognise
Accept means recognise and except means include
Accept means consent and except means apart from
Accept means allude and except means apart from

en.explanation:
Accept: This word is a verb. It means to receive something willingly, to agree to something, or to consider something as true or suitable. Think of it like saying "yes" to receiving something or agreeing with something. A common meaning is 'to consent'.

Except: This word is usually a preposition or conjunction. It means "apart from," "excluding," or "but." It's used to indicate what is not included in a general statement.

Looking at the options provided:

Accept means apart from (No, that's 'except') and except means recognise (No, that's 'accept').

Accept means recognise (Could be, but not the primary or best fit here) and except means include (Definitely not, 'except' means exclude).

Accept means consent (Yes, that's a good fit) and except means apart from (Yes, that's a perfect fit).

Accept means allude (No, 'allude' means to hint at) and except means apart from (Yes, but 'accept' is wrong).

Based on the meanings, Option 3 describes both words most appropriately.

So, the option that correctly describes the meaning of both words is:

Option 3: Accept means consent and except means apart from

en.explanation:
Accord: This word generally means agreement or harmony. Think of reaching an accord on a peace treaty – everyone agrees.

Accrued: This is the past tense of "accrue." It means something has accumulated or gathered over time, often like interest adding up on a loan or benefits building up.

Looking at your options:

Accord means collected and accrued means agreement - Not quite right.

Accord means agreement and accrued means arrived - Accrued isn't about arriving.

Accord means accepted and accrued means received - Accepted is close for accord, but accrued is better described as accumulated.

So, the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both words is Option 4.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items, a pair of words is provided. You are required to select the option that most appropriately describes the meaning of both the words and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: Guarantee and Warranty

options.content: Guarantee is an agreement and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is a business agreement and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract
Guarantee is acceptance and warranty is a service contract

en.explanation:
Think about how "guarantee" and "warranty" are usually used when you buy something.

A guarantee is often a more general assurance or promise from the seller. It's a strong declaration of quality or performance. Like a "money-back guarantee" – that's a straightforward promise. So, "Guarantee is a promise" feels right.

A warranty is typically a specific written agreement that comes with a product. It outlines what the seller will do if the product is defective within a certain time period – often involving repairs or replacement, which are forms of "service." So, thinking of a "warranty" as a type of "service contract" makes sense in this context, as it's a contract promising a remedy (like service) under specific conditions.

Looking at the options:

"Guarantee is an agreement and warranty is a service contract" - Both are agreements, so this isn't very distinguishing for the guarantee.

"Guarantee is a business agreement and warranty is a service contract" - Similar issue as option 1.

"Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract" - This fits well with the common understanding. A guarantee is a strong promise, and a warranty is a contractual promise for potential future service (repair/replacement).

"Guarantee is acceptance and warranty is a service contract" - "Acceptance" doesn't describe what a guarantee is.

Based on how these words are commonly used and legally understood, Option 3 provides the most appropriate description for both terms out of the choices given.

So, the option that best fits is:

$$ \text{Guarantee is a promise and warranty is a service contract} $$

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
en.content: The Constitution of India ensures proportionate representation from all regions.
en.explanation:
Key Points

The term 'proportionate' refers to something that is corresponding in size or amount to something else, forming a proportion. In Hindi, it is termed as "आनुपातिक".

Example: The punishments were proportionate to the crimes committed.

'Balanced' means something that is stable and in fair proportion, suggesting that elements are properly or appropriately distributed. In Hindi, it is "संतुलित".

Example: A balanced diet includes a variety of food groups.

Therefore, the correct answer is "balanced".

Additional Information

Here are explanations for the other options, along with their meanings in Hindi and example sentences:

Partial (आंशिक): Exists only in part; incomplete.

Example: She made a partial payment on her loan.

Unlikely (असंभव): Improbable or not likely to happen.

Example: It is unlikely that he will arrive before noon.

Suffragette (मताधिकार समर्थक महिला): A woman advocating for the right to vote through organized protest.

Example: The suffragettes fought bravely for women's voting rights.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
en.content: There is a feeling of disenchantment among the members of the group.
en.explanation:
That sentence talks about a feeling of disenchantment among the group members.

Let's think about what that word means. When you're "enchanted," you feel charmed or really excited about something, almost like it's magical. So, "disenchantment" is the opposite – it's that feeling when the magic wears off, and you feel let down because things weren't as great or exciting as you thought they would be.

Looking at the options:

delight: That's feeling really happy or pleased, which is the opposite of disenchantment.

disappointment: This is a feeling of sadness or displeasure because your hopes or expectations weren't met. This fits perfectly!

idealism: This is about having high hopes or believing in perfect ideas, but it's not the feeling of being let down.

unrelenting: This means not stopping or easing up, which doesn't relate to a feeling about expectations.

So, the word that's nearest in meaning to disenchantment is disappointment.

The correct option is Option 2.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Synonyms • English
en.excerpt: There is a feeling of disenchantment among the members of the group.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
en.content: She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group.
en.explanation:
The sentence is: "She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group."

The word imminent usually means something that is about to happen very soon. It often carries a sense of being close at hand, perhaps even unavoidable.

Now let's look at the options given:

timely: This means happening at the right or most suitable time. It doesn't quite capture the idea of happening very soon.

distant: This means far away in time or space. This is actually the opposite of imminent.

unlikely: This means not likely to happen. Imminent suggests it is going to happen, and soon, so this is also incorrect.

inevitable: This means certain to happen; unavoidable.

When someone believes something is imminent, especially something like being chosen for a role, they often feel it's not just happening soon, but that it's practically guaranteed or unavoidable at that time. Among the options, "inevitable" is the word that best captures this sense of impending certainty that is often part of what makes something seem "imminent". It suggests that the event is so sure to happen that it's right around the corner.

So, the most appropriate option nearest in meaning to imminent in this context is inevitable.

The correct option is Option 4.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Synonyms • English
en.excerpt: She believed that it was imminent that he would be chosen as the leader of the group.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
en.content: The brave soldiers left an indelible impression on the people of the land.
en.explanation:
Let's think about what "indelible" usually means. It's often used for things like ink that won't wash out, or memories that you can't forget. So, it means something that cannot be removed, washed away, or erased.

Now let's look at the options:

permanent: This means lasting forever or for a very long time, something that stays.

fleeting: This means lasting for a very short time, quickly passing.

hilarious: This means very funny.

eradicable: This means capable of being removed or destroyed.

Comparing "indelible" to these options, the word that is nearest in meaning, especially when talking about an impression that won't go away, is permanent.

So, the most appropriate option is permanent.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Synonyms • English
en.excerpt: The brave soldiers left an indelible impression on the people of the land.

en.comprehension: Directions : In the following items a sentence or a phrase is given with a word that is underlined, followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option that is nearest in meaning to the underlined word and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly
en.content: The manager always provides instantaneous replies to all queries.
en.explanation:
The word "instantaneous" means happening right away, without any delay.

Looking at the options:

Option 1: immediate - This means happening at once, right away.

Option 2: delayed - This means happening later.

Option 3: deliberate - This means done intentionally or planned.

Option 4: unwanted - This means not desired.

Comparing these to "instantaneous," the word that is nearest in meaning is immediate.

So, the most appropriate option is Option 1.

en.comprehension: Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of which comprise a passage. The first and the sixth sentences are fixed in their position as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find out the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the answer sheet.
en.content:
S1: Although all sources of energy commonly come from natural processes, non-renewable resources, are being rapidly depleted and at the rate they are being used.
S6: The sustainable use of natural resources in a manner that provides for resource needs for current resources for humans over a period of time can be termed as con- servation.
P: This will increase the time and cost of mining and once these resources are used up they cannot be replaced.
Q: Hence, we must remember that though our country is rich in natural deposits these resources are not.
R: Extraction of these ores through the process of mining will soon become difficult and also expensive because these minerals are often located deep from greater depths over time.
S: Mineral resources can be said to be finite and non-renewable.
en.explanation:
The sentences slot in as follows:

S1 → S → Q → R → P → S6

That corresponds to option 3, i.e. the order SQRP.

Briefly:

• S defines mineral resources as finite/non-renewable right after the depletion warning in S1.

• Q then draws the conclusion (“these resources are not [infinite]”).

• R explains how that finiteness makes mining difficult and costly as ores lie deeper.

• P adds that time and cost of mining will rise and once gone they can’t be replaced.

• Finally, S6 offers “sustainable use …” (conservation) as the way forward.

en.comprehension: Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of which comprise a passage. The first and the sixth sentences are fixed in their position as S1 and S6. en.content:
S1: National highways connect one state will another and are of national importance.

P: There are important because while others are built to connect 75 per cent of the total road networks, they carry 40 per cent of the total road traffic.

The sentences fit together most coherently in the order S → R → P → Q.

So the complete passage is:

S1: National highways connect one state to another and are of national importance.

S: Since they cover the length and breadth of the country and link cities and towns, these highways are the primary facilitators of trade and connectivity.

R: This is due to the traffic because these roads connect long distances and pass through many cities and towns.

P: They are important because, while other roads account for 75 % of the total network, national highways carry 40 % of the total road traffic.

Q: The infrastructure of the country is therefore crucial, and its development and maintenance are of critical importance.

S6: These road systems are also known as primary road systems and are laid and operated under the National Highway Authority of India.

Answer: Option 2 (SRPQ).

en.comprehension: Directions : In this section each item consists of six sentences of which comprise a passage. The first and the sixth sentences are fixed in their position as S1 and S6. The middle four sentences have been jumbled up and labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to find out the proper sequence of the four sentences and mark your response accordingly on the answer sheet.
en.content:
S1: The South African Constitution was inaugurated in December 1996.
S6: special constitutional court enforces the rights enshrined in the Constitution.
P: Its creation and promulgation took place at a time when South Africa was after the brutal era of the Warfield Government.
Q: The South African Constitution says that its "Bill of Rights is the cornerstone of democracy in South Africa."
R: Apropos, it forbids discrimination on the grounds of "race, gender, sexual orientation, status, ethnic or social origin, colour, age, disability, religion, conscience, belief, culture, language and birth."
S: The Bill of Rights grants perhaps the most extensive range of rights to its citizens.
en.explanation:
S1 introduces the South African Constitution and its inauguration date.

S6 talks about the special court that enforces the rights mentioned in the Constitution.

Now let's look at P, Q, R, and S:

P: Gives historical context for the constitution's creation (after the Warfield Government). This seems like a good follow-up to S1, setting the scene.

Q: Introduces the "Bill of Rights" and calls it the "cornerstone of democracy".

R: Lists specific grounds for forbidding discrimination, which are examples of rights.

S: Says the Bill of Rights grants extensive rights.

Let's try connecting them logically:

S1 sets the stage by mentioning the constitution's inauguration.

P then provides the crucial historical context in which it was created. (S1 -> P)

After introducing the constitution and its context, it makes sense to talk about a key part of it. Both Q and S introduce the Bill of Rights. Q calls it the "cornerstone", and S says it grants "extensive rights". Q stating its significance as a "cornerstone" seems like a good way to introduce its importance, which S can then elaborate on by saying how it's important – by granting extensive rights. (P -> Q -> S)

R then provides specific examples of these extensive rights, particularly the non-discrimination clauses. (S -> R)

Finally, S6 talks about the court that enforces these rights. This follows naturally after discussing the rights themselves.

So, the logical sequence appears to be P, Q, S, R.

Let's put it all together:

S1: The South African Constitution was inaugurated in December 1996.

P: Its creation and promulgation took place at a time when South Africa was after the brutal era of the Warfield Government.

Q: The South African Constitution says that its "Bill of Rights is the cornerstone of democracy in South Africa."

S: The Bill of Rights grants perhaps the most extensive range of rights to its citizens.

R: Apropos, it forbids discrimination on the grounds of "race, gender, sexual orientation, status, ethnic or social origin, colour, age, disability, religion, conscience, belief, culture, language and birth."

S6: special constitutional court enforces the rights enshrined in the Constitution.

This sequence PQSR makes a lot of sense. It starts with the constitution's inauguration, provides context, introduces the Bill of Rights and its significance (cornerstone), describes its scope (extensive rights), gives specific examples (non-discrimination), and ends by mentioning the enforcement mechanism.

Looking at the options provided:

Option 1: QSRP

Option 2: SPQR

Option 3: PQRS

Option 4: PQSR

Our derived sequence PQSR matches Option 4.

The final answer is $\boxed{PQSR}$.

en.comprehension: Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker/expression and mark your answer accordingly in the Answer Sheet :
en.content: __________________ your transfer request is concerned, we'll be discussing it at the next meeting.
en.explanation:
Explanation:

The correct discourse marker for the sentence is "As far as."

Key Points:

The phrase "As far as…is concerned" is a widely accepted and grammatically correct discourse marker used to introduce a specific topic or aspect. It indicates that the subsequent statement pertains directly to that subject.

In the context of the sentence, it specifies that the transfer request will be addressed in the upcoming meeting.

Therefore, the correct answer is Option 1.

Complete Sentence:

As far as your transfer request is concerned, we'll be discussing it at the next meeting.

Additional Information:

Option 2: "To begin with" is employed to introduce the initial point or topic in a series, or to state an initial condition. It doesn't align with the structure "…is concerned."

Option 3: "By the way" is typically used to introduce information that is off-topic or tangential to the current discussion. It does not match the structure "…is concerned."

Option 4: "Talking about" introduces a related topic or a new subject. While it serves to introduce a topic, it doesn't fit the grammatical construct "…is concerned." Instead, you would say, "Talking about your transfer request, we'll discuss it…"

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Sentence Completion • English
en.excerpt: __________________ your transfer request is concerned, we'll be discussing it at the next meeting.

en.comprehension: Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker/expression and mark your answer accordingly in the Answer Sheet :
en.content: ___________________, did you know Ravi has a new job ?
en.explanation:
"_______, did you know Ravi has a new job?"

We need something that smoothly introduces a new topic, almost like a little detour in the conversation.

Think about the options:

For one thing: We usually use this when we're listing reasons or points about something. Like, "Why don't you like the movie? For one thing, the acting was terrible." It doesn't quite fit for just dropping some news.

By the way: Ah, this one is perfect for bringing up something new or slightly unrelated that just popped into your head during a conversation. Like, "We were talking about the party… by the way, did you know Ravi has a new job?" It shifts the focus naturally.

In the first place: This is another one we use for listing reasons, often for the initial point. Like, "Why didn't you go? In the first place, I was too busy." Not quite right here.

As a result: This shows cause and effect. "He studied hard; as a result, he passed the exam." Definitely doesn't fit introducing news.

So, the one that feels most natural for suddenly asking if someone knows about Ravi's new job is By the way. It signals a friendly shift to a different topic.

Option 2, By the way, is the best fit!

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Sentence Completion • English
en.excerpt: ___________________, did you know Ravi has a new job ?

en.comprehension: Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker/expression and mark your answer accordingly in the Answer Sheet :
en.content: __________________ the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder.
en.explanation:
The sentence is talking about the wind and how it's affecting the speaker. We need a phrase to smoothly introduce the wind as the subject being discussed, leading into the description of it getting stronger and making the speaker colder.

Let's consider the choices:

To begin with: This is usually used to start a discussion or list the first point of something. It doesn't quite fit starting a sentence about an ongoing condition like the wind.

By the way: This is used to introduce a new topic or add extra information that isn't directly related to what was just said. The wind is the main subject here, not an aside.

First of all: Similar to "to begin with," it's for introducing the initial point or item. Not the right fit here.

Speaking of: Ah, this one is often used to transition to a topic that is currently relevant or has just been mentioned (even if implicitly by the situation). If you are out in the wind, and you want to comment on it, saying "Speaking of the wind…" works perfectly to introduce your observation.

Looking at the sentence, "__ the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder," option 4, "Speaking of," flows naturally. It introduces the wind as the topic you are about to discuss further.

So, the most appropriate discourse marker is "Speaking of".

The completed sentence would be:

Speaking of the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder.

This makes sense because you are bringing up the topic of the wind to explain why you are getting colder and noting its increasing strength.

So, the correct option is Option 4.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Sentence Completion • English
en.excerpt: __________________ the wind, it was getting stronger, and I was getting colder.

en.comprehension: Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker/expression and mark your answer accordingly in the Answer Sheet :
en.content: _________________ what are you doing in my room ?
en.explanation:
The sentence "_ what are you doing in my room?" sounds like someone is a bit surprised or perhaps even slightly annoyed to find someone there. They need a way to grab attention or preface their direct question.

On the other hand: We use this when we're talking about a contrasting point. Like, "This cake is delicious; on the other hand, it's full of sugar." That doesn't quite fit here, does it?

Excuse me: People often say "excuse me" to get someone's attention, to interrupt politely, or even to express a bit of surprise or disbelief about something unexpected. If you walk into your room and find someone there, starting with "Excuse me" is a very common and natural way to lead into the question of why they're there.

Look there: This is used to point something out visually, like "Look there, a rainbow!" It doesn't work as an introduction to a question about someone's presence.

Eventually: This word talks about something happening after some time, like "Eventually, the sun set." That doesn't fit the immediate situation of finding someone in your room.

Looking at these, "Excuse me" feels like the perfect fit! It smoothly introduces the question and reflects that slight surprise or need to get their attention before asking the direct question.

So, the most appropriate discourse marker is Excuse me.

en.title: NDA General Ability 13 April 2025
en.subTitle: Sentence Completion • English
en.excerpt: _________________ what are you doing in my room ?

en.comprehension: Directions : Given below are sentences that use discourse markers/expressions to make them complete. Identify the most appropriate discourse marker/expression and mark your answer accordingly in the Answer Sheet :
en.content: _________________ an unhappy childhood leads to criminal behaviour.
en.explanation:
Key Points

The phrase "In some cases" is a discourse marker used to indicate that something is true in certain instances or situations, but not necessarily in all or most cases. It conveys a sense of partial truth or occasional occurrence.

The sentence suggests a correlation between an unhappy childhood and criminal behavior, but it's understood that this isn't a universal or absolute rule. "In some cases" perfectly captures this nuance, implying that while it can happen, it's not a definitive outcome for everyone with an unhappy childhood.

Therefore, the correct choice is Option 3.

Complete Sentence: "In some cases an unhappy childhood leads to criminal behaviour."

Additional Information

Option 1: "Still if" is grammatically awkward and doesn't convey the intended meaning of occasional occurrence.

Option 2: "Even somewhat" is also grammatically incorrect in this context and does not function as an appropriate discourse marker here.

Option 4: "In progression" implies a sequence or a development over time. Although an unhappy childhood might be part of a progression, "In progression an unhappy childhood leads…" is not a natural or idiomatic way to frame this statement.

options.content: Person of roughly the same age
Person or organisation that cooperates with others
Person employed to drive a private car

en.explanation:
The term Coeval is defined as (especially concerning people or things) existing or occurring at the same time; of the same age.

Example: The dinosaurs and the first mammals were coeval.

Person of roughly the same age clearly represents 'Coeval' when referring to people.

Example: My coevals and I share many similar memories of that decade.

Thus, the correct meaning of "Coeval" is a person of roughly the same age.

Additional Information

Person or organisation that cooperates with others: Refers to a collaborator or ally.

Person employed to drive a private car: Known as a chauffeur.

Person employed in taking census: Describes a census taker or enumerator.

An "imprest" or "imprest fund" is essentially a fixed amount of money set aside, often called petty cash, which is used for covering minor everyday expenses in a business or organization. When money is spent from the fund, it's replenished back to the original amount.

en.content:
participate in the function     many of the people who had come to
                       P                                     Q
the auditorium was over-crowded            could not find a seat because
                 R                                                                   S
en.explanation:
We need to figure out the best order for P, Q, R, and S.

Looking at the parts:

Q: "many of the people who had come to" - This looks like the beginning, introducing the subject.

P: "participate in the function" - This tells us why the people came. It fits well after "come to". So, QP makes sense: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function".

S: "could not find a seat because" - This tells us what happened to those people. It follows naturally after describing the people. So, QPS: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function could not find a seat because".

R: "the auditorium was over-crowded" - This gives the reason for why they couldn't find a seat. It fits perfectly after "because". So, QPSR: "many of the people who had come to participate in the function could not find a seat because the auditorium was over-crowded".

This order, QPSR, creates a complete and logical sentence.

Now let's check the options:

Option 1: PRQS - Doesn't fit.

Option 2: PQRS - Doesn't fit.

Option 3: QPSR - Yes, this matches our logical order.

Option 4: SPRQ - Doesn't fit.

So, the most appropriate arrangement is QPSR.

The correct option is Option 3.

en.content:
lay outside her existing life           she knew that the answer
               P                                                      Q 
somewhere in the back of her mind             to her questions about life
                      R                                                              S
en.explanation:
The correct answer is Option (4) i.e. RQSP

Key Points

The sentence illustrates a realization occurring in someone's thoughts.

We begin with the adverbial phrase that identifies the location of this knowledge: "somewhere in the back of her mind" (R).

This is followed by the main clause: "she knew that the answer" (Q), thus forming RQ so far.

Next, we clarify what the answer pertained to: "to her questions about life" (S), extending the thought to RQS.

Finally, we describe where the answer was found: "lay outside her existing life" (P). This completes the sentence as RQSP.

Therefore, the correct rearrangement is RQSP.

Additional Information

Option 1 (PRQS): "lay outside her existing life somewhere in the back of her mind she knew that the answer to her questions about life" - This order results in a sentence that is grammatically incorrect and confusing.

Option 2 (QSPR): "she knew that the answer to her questions about life lay outside her existing life somewhere in the back of her mind" - Although grammatically possible, placing "somewhere in the back of her mind" at the end makes the sentence appear less natural and clear compared to starting with it.

Option 3 (PSRQ): "lay outside her existing life to her questions about life somewhere in the back of her mind she knew that the answer" - This sequence is grammatically fragmented and does not make sense.

en.comprehension: Directions : Each of the following items in this section consists of a sentence, the parts of which have been jumbled. These parts have been labelled as P, Q, R and S. You are required to rearrange these jumbled parts, which are labelled as (a), (b), (c) and (d). You are required to select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content:
takes on account of its economic successes           to address continuing social inequality.
                              P                                                                                   Q
for every stride of progress any nation                    it takes two back if it is unable
                               R                                                                S
en.explanation:
P: takes on account of its economic successes

Q: to address continuing social inequality.

R: for every stride of progress any nation

S: it takes two back if it is unable

We need to find the best sequence that makes a complete thought. Let's try building the sentence piece by piece.

Often, sentences start with a general idea or a subject. R seems like a good candidate for the beginning: "for every stride of progress any nation…"

What does a nation do? It "takes" a stride of progress. So, R could naturally be followed by P: "for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes…"

Now, what happens "for every stride" it takes? The phrase "it takes two back" in S suggests a consequence or a setback despite progress. The "it" in S refers back to "any nation". So, S should likely follow R P: "for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes it takes two back if it is unable…"

Finally, unable to do what? The last piece Q tells us: "…to address continuing social inequality." This fits perfectly after the "if it is unable" part of S.

So, putting it all together, the order R P S Q seems to form a coherent sentence:

"for every stride of progress any nation takes on account of its economic successes it takes two back if it is unable to address continuing social inequality."

Let's check this order against the options provided. The order R P S Q corresponds to Option 3.

The most appropriate arrangement is RPSQ.

en.content:
the book-value of a loan or                 an intangible asset over a set period of time
                  P                                                                     Q
used to periodically lower                      amortisation is an accounting technique
                  R                                                                      S
en.explanation:
The correct sequence of the sentence is Option (1): SRPQ.

Key Points

The sentence is providing a definition of "amortisation."

First, we identify the main subject and its initial description: "amortisation is an accounting technique" (S).

Next, we describe how this technique is "used to periodically lower" (R), forming SR.

We then specify what is being lowered: "the book-value of a loan or" (P), extending it to SRP.

Finally, we complete the definition by adding "an intangible asset over a set period of time" (Q), leading to the full sequence: SRPQ.

Therefore, the correct answer is SRPQ.

Additional Information

Option 2 (RSPQ): "used to periodically lower amortisation is an accounting technique the book-value of a loan or an intangible asset over a set period of time" – The sequence is incorrect and grammatically disjointed.

Option 3 (SRQP): "amortisation is an accounting technique used to periodically lower an intangible asset over a set period of time the book-value of a loan or" – The order of P and Q is swapped, resulting in incorrect phrasing.

Option 4 (RPSQ): "used to periodically lower the book-value of a loan or amortisation is an accounting technique an intangible asset over a set period of time" – This sequence is grammatically incorrect and does not form a coherent definition.

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a blank space followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: There is more than ___________________ to get the job done.
en.explanation:
The most appropriate option to complete the sentence is "one way".

Here's why:

The structure "more than one" is usually followed by a singular noun, even though it implies plurality (more than one thing). It's a bit of a linguistic quirk, but that's the standard form in English.

Think of it like this:

"More than one person is here." (not "are here")

"More than one book was lost." (not "were lost")

So, following that pattern, it's "more than one way".

Let's look at why the others don't quite fit:

"one ways": This is grammatically incorrect because "one" needs a singular noun ("way").

"any ways" or "any way": While "any" can relate to options, the phrase "more than any way" or "more than any ways" isn't the standard way to express that there are multiple methods. The common idiom uses "more than one".

So, the completed sentence is: "There is more than one way to get the job done."

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a blank space followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: The class decided to organize__________
en.explanation:
When talking about organizing a single event like this for the first time, or just mentioning the act in general, we usually use the indefinite article "a" before the noun.

Think about it like this:

You'd say "I want to buy a book."

You'd say "They decided to build a house."

Similarly, for "picnic," if it's a single, unspecified one, you'd say "organize a picnic."

Using "picnic" alone sounds a bit off for a countable noun like this. "Picnics" would mean they decided to organize more than one. "The picnic" would imply there's a specific picnic everyone already knows about.

So, the most natural fit is Option 2: a picnic.

en.content: The accused ___________________ before the judge.
en.explanation:
The sentence is describing what happens to someone accused of a crime when they are in court with a judge.

"tried" - While the accused is tried, the phrasing "The accused tried before the judge" isn't the standard way to say it in English. It would usually be passive, like "The accused was tried…"

"was trial" - "Trial" is a noun, so this doesn't work grammatically with "was".

"sat trial" - People do sit in court, but "sat trial" isn't a common or correct legal phrase.

"stood trial" - This is a very common and correct legal idiom! "To stand trial" means to undergo a trial in a court of law.

So, the most appropriate option to fill in the blank is stood trial.

The full sentence would be: The accused stood trial before the judge.

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a blank space followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out _______________ scrutiny by the HIgh court.
en.explanation:
"The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out ___ scrutiny by the High Court."

We need a phrase that shows the relationship between "The manner" and "scrutiny by the High Court". This relationship seems to be a current state – that the way something was done is presently being examined or is subject to examination by the court.

Let's think about the options:

was under: This would mean the scrutiny happened in the past, and perhaps it's finished now.

under: This word needs a verb before it to make sense in this structure (like "is under" or "was under"). It can't stand alone here.

is under: This means the scrutiny is happening right now. The way the demolitions were done is currently being examined by the High Court. This seems to fit the idea of a present legal situation.

however under: "However" is used to show contrast, and like just "under", it's not the main verb needed here.

Thinking about what the sentence likely means – that the High Court is currently looking into how the demolitions happened – the phrase that best expresses this ongoing state is "is under".

So, the most appropriate option to fill in the blank is Option 3: is under.

The complete sentence would be: "The manner in which the demolitions have been carried out is under scrutiny by the High Court."

en.comprehension: Directions : The following items have a blank space followed by four options. Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank and mark your response on the Answer Sheet accordingly.
en.content: I ___________________ consider the opinions of all stakeholders before taking a final decision.
en.explanation:
The sentence is: "I _______ consider the opinions of all stakeholders before taking a final decision."

We need a word that shows what you will do regarding the opinions. Let's look at the options:

will not: If you use "will not," the sentence becomes "I will not consider the opinions…", which means you won't think about what the stakeholders think. That changes the whole meaning!

have: If you use "have," the sentence becomes "I have consider the opinions…". This isn't grammatically correct. You'd need "have considered" for the past tense.

will: If you use "will," the sentence becomes "I will consider the opinions…". This is grammatically correct and shows your intention to consider the opinions in the future.

shall: If you use "shall," the sentence becomes "I shall consider the opinions…". This is also grammatically correct and shows your intention to consider the opinions. Often, "shall" with "I" or "we" suggests a strong intention, a promise, or a statement of what is intended to happen. It can sound a bit more formal or determined than "will".

Both "will" and "shall" work grammatically and convey a future action. However, in the context of making a formal decision involving stakeholders, stating "I shall consider…" often implies a stronger sense of determination or commitment to that process.

Given the options and the context, shall (Option 4) feels like the most appropriate choice, suggesting a clear and determined intention to follow this crucial step in the decision-making process.

So, the most appropriate option is shall.

en.content: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?

options.content: The molecules of soap are sodium or potassium salts of long chain fatty acids
The molecules of soap contain both hydrophobic and hydrophilic ends
Detergents are more effective than soaps in hard water
In micelles the ionic-end of the molecules is towards oil droplet while the other end faces outside

en.explanation:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 4.

Reason: In a micelle formed in water, the ionic (hydrophilic) ends face the surrounding aqueous medium, while the non-polar (hydrophobic) tails point inward toward the trapped oil or grease. Option 4 has this orientation reversed.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

options.content: साबुन के अपूर्ण दीर्घ शृंखला वाले वसा-अम्लों के सोडियम या पोटैशियम लवण हैं
साबुन अपूर्ण में जलरागी और जलविरागी दोनों सिरे होते हैं
कठोर जल में साबुन की तुलना में अपमार्जक अधिक प्रभावकारी होते हैं
मिस्र में अपूर्णों का आकर्षक-सिरा तेल की की ओर होता है, जबकि दूसरा सिरा बाहर की ओर होता है

hi.explanation:
जो कथन सही नहीं है, वह विकल्प 4 है।

कारण: जल में बनने वाले माइसेल में, आयनिक (जलस्नेही) सिरे चारों ओर स्थित जलीय माध्यम की ओर होते हैं, जबकि गैर-ध्रुवीय (जलविरोधी) पूंछें अंदर की ओर फंसी हुई तेल या चिकनाई की ओर होती हैं। विकल्प 4 में इस अभिविन्यास को उल्टा दर्शाया गया है।

en.content: Which one of the following statements is correct ?

options.content: A neutron is formed by combination of an electron and a proton. Therefore, it is neutral
The mass of an electron is about 1/2000 times that of a proton
An isotope of cobalt is used in the treatment of goiter
J. J. Thomson proposed that the nucleus of an atom contains only neutrons

en.explanation:
Option 2 is correct.

Explanation:

The mass of an electron is

$$m_e \approx 9.11\times10^{-31}\,\text{kg},$$

while that of a proton is

$$m_p \approx 1.67\times10^{-27}\,\text{kg}.$$

Their ratio is

$$\frac{m_e}{m_p}\approx\frac{1}{1836}\approx\frac{1}{2000}.$$

Why the others are wrong:

Option 1 describes an outdated idea; neutrons aren’t simply proton+electron combinations.

Option 3 mixes up treatments: radioiodine isotopes help treat goiter, not cobalt.

Option 4 is incorrect—J. J. Thomson’s model didn’t place neutrons in the nucleus (neutrons were discovered later by Chadwick).

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही है?

options.content: न्यूट्रॉन एक इलेक्ट्रॉन और एक प्रोटोन के संयोजन से बनता है। इसलिए यह उदासीन (न्यूट्रल) होता है
एक इलेक्ट्रॉन का द्रव्यमान, एक प्रोटोन के द्रव्यमान का लगभग 1/2000 गुणा होता है
कोबाल्ट के एक समस्थानिक का उपयोग घेंघा (Goiter) के उपचार में किया जाता है
जे. जे. थॉमसन ने प्रस्तावित किया कि परमाणु के नाभिक में केवल न्यूट्रॉन होते हैं

hi.explanation:
विकल्प 2 सही है।

व्याख्या:

इलेक्ट्रॉन का द्रव्यमान है

$$m_e \approx 9.11\times10^{-31}\,\text{kg},$$

जबकि प्रोटॉन का द्रव्यमान है

$$m_p \approx 1.67\times10^{-27}\,\text{kg}.$$

इन दोनों का अनुपात है

$$\frac{m_e}{m_p}\approx\frac{1}{1836}\approx\frac{1}{2000}.$$

अन्य विकल्प गलत क्यों हैं:

विकल्प 1 एक पुरानी धारणा का वर्णन करता है; न्यूट्रॉन केवल प्रोटॉन और इलेक्ट्रॉन का संयोजन नहीं है।

विकल्प 3 अवधारणा में गड़बड़ी है: घेंघा (goiter) के उपचार में रेडियोआयोडीन आयसोटोप्स का उपयोग होता है, न कि कोबाल्ट का।

विकल्प 4 गलत है—जे. जे. थॉमसन के मॉडल में न्यूट्रॉन का नाभिक में स्थान नहीं था (न्यूट्रॉन की खोज बाद में चैडविक द्वारा की गई थी)।

en.content: Which one of the following particles in the nucleus of an atom was discovered by J. Chadwick?
en.explanation:
Looking at the history of particle discoveries:

The electron was discovered by J.J. Thomson.

The proton was identified by Ernest Rutherford.

The positron was discovered by Carl Anderson.

The neutron was discovered by James Chadwick.

And among the particles listed, the electron is found outside the nucleus, while protons and neutrons are found inside the nucleus. The positron is an antiparticle and not a standard part of the nucleus.

So, the particle in the nucleus discovered by J. Chadwick is the Neutron. That's option 4!

Chadwick's discovery in 1932 was super important for understanding the atom. He found this neutral particle that had a mass very close to that of a proton, which helped explain the mass of atoms that couldn't just be accounted for by protons alone.

hi.content: जे. चैडविक ने परमाणु के नाभिक में, निम्नलिखित में से किस कण की खोज की थी?
hi.explanation:
कणों की खोज के इतिहास को देखते हुए:

इलेक्ट्रॉन की खोज जे. जे. थॉमसन द्वारा की गई थी।

प्रोटॉन की पहचान अर्नेस्ट रदरफोर्ड द्वारा की गई थी।

पॉजिट्रॉन की खोज कार्ल एंडरसन ने की थी।

न्यूट्रॉन की खोज जेम्स चैडविक ने की थी।

और सूचीबद्ध कणों में, इलेक्ट्रॉन नाभिक के बाहर पाया जाता है, जबकि प्रोटॉन और न्यूट्रॉन नाभिक के अंदर पाए जाते हैं। पॉजिट्रॉन एक प्रतिकण (antiparticle) है और नाभिक का सामान्य घटक नहीं है।

तो, वह कण जो नाभिक में पाया जाता है और जिसकी खोज जे. चैडविक ने की थी, वह न्यूट्रॉन है। यही विकल्प 4 है!

चैडविक की 1932 में की गई खोज परमाणु को समझने के लिए अत्यंत महत्त्वपूर्ण थी। उन्होंने इस तटस्थ कण को खोजा जिसका द्रव्यमान प्रोटॉन के लगभग बराबर था, जिससे ऐसे परमाणुओं के द्रव्यमान की व्याख्या करना संभव हुआ जिन्हें केवल प्रोटॉनों के द्वारा ही नहीं समझाया जा सकता था।

en.content: Which one of the following findings is NOT observed in Rutherford's Nuclear Model of Atom?

options.content: There is a neutral centre in an atom called nucleus
Nearly all the mass of an atom resides in the nucleus
The electrons revolve around the nucleus in a circular path
The size of a nucleus is very small as compared to the size of atom

en.explanation:
The statement NOT supported by Rutherford’s model is:

Option 1: “There is a neutral centre in an atom called nucleus.”

Rutherford’s gold-foil experiment showed the nucleus is extremely small, contains nearly all the atom’s mass and carries a positive charge. Electrons do revolve around it in the surrounding empty space.

hi.content: रदरफोर्ड के नाभिकीय परमाणु मॉडल से निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा निष्कर्ष नहीं निकाला गया है?

options.content: परमाणु में एक उदासीन केंद्र होता है जिसे नाभिक कहते हैं
परमाणु का लगभग संपूर्ण द्रव्यमान नाभिक में होता है
इलेक्ट्रॉन नाभिक के चारों ओर वृत्ताकार पथ पर घूमते हैं
नाभिक का आकार परमाणु के आकार की तुलना में अत्यधिक छोटा होता है

hi.explanation:
रदरफोर्ड के मॉडल द्वारा समर्थित नहीं होने वाला कथन है:

विकल्प 1: “परमाणु के केंद्र में एक उदासीन (न्यूट्रल) केंद्र होता है, जिसे न्यूक्लियस कहते हैं।”

रदरफोर्ड के स्वर्ण-पत्र प्रयोग (Gold Foil Experiment) ने दर्शाया कि न्यूक्लियस अत्यंत छोटा होता है, जिसमें परमाणु का लगभग संपूर्ण द्रव्यमान संचित रहता है और यह धनावेशित (सकारात्मक आवेशित) होता है। इसके चारों ओर की रिक्त स्थान में इलेक्ट्रॉन घूमते रहते हैं।

en.content: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
en.explanation:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

hi.content: तत्व X एक क्लोराइड बनाता है, जिसका सूत्र XCl₂ है, जो उच्च गलनांक वाला एक ठोस है। रासायनिक संकेतक: X आवर्त सारणी के उसी समूह में होगा जिसमें निम्नलिखित है
hi.explanation:
XCl₂ का उच्च गलनांक वाला ठोस होना यह दर्शाता है कि यह एक आयनिक यौगिक है जिसमें X +2 ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था में है। वे धातुएँ जो सामान्यतः MCl₂ लवण बनाती हैं, वे समूह 2 (क्षारीय पृथ्वी) के तत्त्व होते हैं।

• समूह 2 के तत्त्व → बेरीलियम (Be), मैग्नीशियम (Mg), कैल्शियम (Ca), …

• दिये गए विकल्पों में से केवल Mg ही समूह 2 की धातु है।

उत्तर: विकल्प 3 (Mg)

en.content: Which one of the following oxides is a neutral oxide?
en.explanation:
The neutral oxide among the given choices is carbon monoxide, CO.

• CO₂ is acidic (forms H₂CO₃ in water)

• Na₂O and MgO are basic (react with water to give OH⁻)

• CO does not react appreciably with acids or bases, so it’s considered neutral.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित ऑक्साइडों में से कौन-सा एक उदासीन ऑक्साइड है?
hi.explanation:
दिए गए विकल्पों में तटस्थ ऑक्साइड कार्बन मोनोक्साइड (CO) है।

• CO₂ अम्लीय है (जल में H₂CO₃ बनाता है)

• Na₂O तथा MgO क्षारीय हैं (जल के साथ अभिक्रिया कर OH⁻ उत्पन्न करते हैं)

• CO न तो अम्लों के साथ और न ही क्षारों के साथ उल्लेखनीय रूप से अभिक्रिया करता है, इसलिए इसे तटस्थ माना जाता है।

hi.excerpt: निम्नलिखित ऑक्साइडों में से कौन-सा एक उदासीन ऑक्साइड है?

en.content: Which one of the following is also known as Dry ice in its solid form?
en.explanation:
Dry ice is actually the solid form of a very common gas. The compound known as dry ice is carbon dioxide.

Its chemical formula is $$CO_2$$.

Looking at the options you gave:

Option 1: $$SiO_2$$ is Silicon dioxide, which is more commonly known as silica.

Option 2: $$CO_2$$ is Carbon dioxide.

Option 3: $$CaO$$ is Calcium oxide, or quicklime.

Option 4: $$MgO$$ is Magnesium oxide.

So, the one that is also known as dry ice in its solid form is $$CO_2$$, Carbon dioxide.

That means Option 2 is the correct one! Dry ice gets its name because it doesn't melt into a liquid; instead, it goes directly from a solid to a gas, a process called sublimation.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से किसे, ठोस रूप में होने पर शुष्क बर्फ के रूप में भी जाना जाता है?
hi.explanation:
ड्राई आइस वास्तव में एक बहुत सामान्य गैस का ठोस रूप है। ड्राई आइस के रूप में जाना जाने वाला यौगिक कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड है।

इसका रासायनिक सूत्र $$CO_2$$ है।

आपके द्वारा दिए गए विकल्पों को देखते हैं:

विकल्प 1: $$SiO_2$$ सिलिका या सिलिकॉन डाइऑक्साइड है, जिसे सामान्यतः सिलिका कहा जाता है।

विकल्प 2: $$CO_2$$ कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड है।

विकल्प 3: $$CaO$$ कैल्शियम ऑक्साइड है, जिसे क्विक लाइम भी कहते हैं।

विकल्प 4: $$MgO$$ मैग्नीशियम ऑक्साइड है।

इसलिए, ठोस रूप में ड्राई आइस के नाम से भी पहचाना जाने वाला यौगिक $$CO_2$$, अर्थात् कार्बन डाइऑक्साइड है।

इसका अर्थ है कि विकल्प 2 सही है! ड्राई आइस को यह नाम इसलिए दिया गया है क्योंकि यह पिघलकर द्रव नहीं बनता, बल्कि सीधे ठोस से गैस में बदल जाता है, जिसे 'उध्रुवीकरण' (sublimation) कहते हैं।

en.content: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.content: A scale measuring hydrogen ion concentration in a solution is called pH scale
The higher the hydrogen ion concentration in a solution, higher is its pH
We can measure pH generally from 0 to 14 on a pH scale
The 'p' in pH stands for 'potenz' in German meaning power

en.explanation:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 2.

Explanation:

By definition,

$$\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+].$$

If $[H^+]$ increases, $\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes up, so $\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+]$ goes down.

Hence, a higher hydrogen‐ion concentration actually means a lower pH, not a higher one.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

options.content: किसी विलयन में हाइड्रोजन आयन की सांद्रता मापने वाले पैमाने को pH स्केल कहा जाता है
किसी विलयन में हाइड्रोजन आयन की सांद्रता जितनी अधिक होगी, उसका pH मान उतना ही अधिक होगा
हम आम तौर पर pH पैमाने पर pH को 0 से 14 तक माप सकते हैं
pH में 'p' का अर्थ जर्मन में 'पोटेंज़' होता है जिसका अर्थ है शक्ति

hi.explanation:
जो कथन सही नहीं है, वह विकल्प 2 है।

व्याख्या:

परिभाषा के अनुसार,

$$\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+].$$

यदि $[H^+]$ बढ़ता है, तो $\log_{10}[H^+]$ भी बढ़ेगा, जिससे $\mathrm{pH} = -\log_{10}[H^+]$ घट जाएगा।

इसलिए, अधिक हाइड्रोजन आयन सांद्रता का अर्थ है कम pH, अधिक नहीं।

en.content: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?

options.content: Half mole of nitrogen gas is measured 11·2 litre at STP
17 gram of ammonia gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules at STP
22·4 litre of CO₂ gas at STP contains 44 gram of molecules
4 gram of hydrogen gas contains 6·022 × 10²³ molecules

en.explanation:
Let’s check each statement using the molar volume (1 mol gas at STP = 22.4 L) and molar masses:

½ mol N₂ → 0.5×22.4 L = 11.2 L ✅

17 g NH₃ (M = 17 g/mol) → 1 mol → 6.022×10²³ molecules ✅

22.4 L CO₂ → 1 mol → 44 g CO₂ ✅

4 g H₂ (M = 2 g/mol) →

$$n=\frac{4}{2}=2\;\text{mol}$$

$$N=2\times6.022\times10^{23}=1.2044\times10^{24}\;\text{molecules}$$

Statement says only 6.022×10²³ molecules, so it’s wrong. ❌

Answer: Option 4 is NOT correct.

hi.content: निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

options.content: STP पर नाइट्रोजन गैस का आधा मोल 11·2 लीटर मापा जाता है
17 ग्राम अमोनिया गैस में STP पर 6·022 × 10²³ अणु होते हैं
STP पर 22·4 लीटर CO₂ गैस में 44 ग्राम अणु होते हैं
4 ग्राम हाइड्रोजन गैस में 6·022 × 10²³ अणु होते हैं

hi.explanation:
आइए प्रत्येक कथन की जाँच करें, मोलर आयतन (सामान्य ताप व दाब पर 1 मोल गैस = 22.4 L) एवं मोलर द्रव्यमान का उपयोग करते हुए:

½ मोल N2 → 0.5×22.4 L = 11.2 L ✅

17 g NH3 (M = 17 g/mol) → 1 मोल → 6.022×1023 अणु ✅

22.4 L CO2 → 1 मोल → 44 g CO2 ✅

4 g H2 (M = 2 g/mol) →

$$n=\frac{4}{2}=2\;\text{mol}$$

$$N=2\times6.022\times10^{23}=1.2044\times10^{24}\;\text{अणु}$$

कथन में केवल 6.022×1023 अणु कहा गया है, अतः यह गलत है। ❌

उत्तर: विकल्प 4 सही नहीं है।

en.content: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?

options.content: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions

en.explanation:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.

hi.content: किसी यौगिक के बारे में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

options.content: यौगिक दो या दो से अधिक तत्वों से बना पदार्थ है, जो एक निश्चित अनुपात में रासायनिक रूप से संयोजित होते हैं
किसी यौगिक के गुण उसके घटक तत्वों से भिन्न होते हैं
किसी यौगिक के घटकों को केवल रासायनिक या विद्युत-रासायनिक अभिक्रियाओं द्वारा ही पृथक किया जा सकता है

hi.explanation:
जो कथन सही नहीं है, वह विकल्प 3 है:

“एक यौगिक अशुद्ध पदार्थ है।”

• परिभाषा के अनुसार, एक यौगिक एक शुद्ध पदार्थ है जो दो या दो से अधिक तत्वों के रासायनिक रूप से निश्चित अनुपात में संयोजन से बनता है।

• इसकी गुणधर्म इसके घटक तत्वों से भिन्न होते हैं।

• इसके संघटकों को केवल रासायनिक या वैद्युत-रासायनिक विधियों द्वारा ही अलग किया जा सकता है।

hi.excerpt: किसी यौगिक के बारे में, निम्नलिखित में से कौन-सा कथन सही नहीं है?

en.content:
Match List I with List II and select the answer using the code given below the Lists:
List I (Chemical Name)  List II (Chemical Formula)
A. Washing soda  1. CaOCl₂
B. Baking soda 2. NaHCO₃
C. Bleaching powder  3. Na₂CO₃·10H₂O
D. Gypsum 4. CaSO₄·2H₂O

Code:
en.explanation:
Here’s how the chemicals match up:

Washing soda (A) is sodium carbonate decahydrate:

$$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 3

Baking soda (B) is sodium bicarbonate:

$$\text{NaHCO}_3$$ → 2

Bleaching powder (C) is calcium oxychloride:

$$\text{CaOCl}_2$$ → 1

Gypsum (D) is calcium sulfate dihydrate:

$$\text{CaSO}_4\cdot2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 4

So the correct code is Option 4:

(a)–3, (b)–2, (c)–1, (d)–4

hi.content:
सूची I को सूची II से सुमेलित करें और सूचियों के नीचे दिए गए कूट का प्रयोग कर उत्तर चुनिए:
सूची I (रासायनिक नाम) सूची II (रासायनिक सूत्र)
A. धावन सोडा 1. CaOCl₂
B. बेकिंग सोडा 2. NaHCO₃
C. ब्लीचिंग पाउडर 3. Na₂CO₃·10H₂O
D. जिप्सम 4. CaSO₄·2H₂O

कोड:
hi.explanation:
रासायनिक पदार्थों का निम्नलिखित प्रकार से मिलान किया गया है:

वॉशिंग सोडा (A) सोडियम कार्बोनेट डिहाइड्रेट है:

$$\text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 3

बेकिंग सोडा (B) सोडियम हाइड्रोजनकार्बोनेट है:

$$\text{NaHCO}_3$$ → 2

ब्लीचिंग पाउडर (C) कैल्शियम ऑक्सीक्लोराइड है:

$$\text{CaOCl}_2$$ → 1

जिप्सम (D) कैल्शियम सल्फेट डाइहाइड्रेट है:

$$\text{CaSO}_4\cdot2\text{H}_2\text{O}$$ → 4

इस प्रकार सही कूट विकल्प 4 है:

(a)–3, (b)–2, (c)–1, (d)–4
